title: Create Mobile Estimates in GoHighLevel — Save Time & Stay Consistent
description: Learn how to create professional mobile estimates in GoHighLevel. Use templates to send branded quotes instantly from your phone and close deals faster.
html_content:
Your field team is out on jobs. A prospect asks for an estimate. Your sales rep pulls out a laptop, drives back to the office, or waits until end of day to send a quote. Days pass. The deal goes cold.

This is the reality for agencies and service businesses without mobile estimate capabilities. But it doesn't have to be.

GoHighLevel's mobile estimate templates solve this exact problem. Your team can now generate professional, branded estimates directly from their phones—in minutes, not hours. They maintain pricing consistency, attach custom terms, collect payments, and track everything in one platform.

In this guide, I'll walk you through how to set up and use mobile estimates in GoHighLevel to eliminate delays, close faster, and keep your brand consistent across every client interaction. And if you're ready to streamline your entire sales process, start your free 30-day trial of GoHighLevel—double the standard 14-day trial.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Access Estimate Templates on the GoHighLevel Mobile App
  • Setting Up Your First Estimate Template
  • Creating and Sending Estimates from Your Phone
  • Key Benefits of Mobile Estimate Templates
  • Best Practices for Field Teams

How to Access Estimate Templates on the GoHighLevel Mobile App

Mobile estimates in GoHighLevel are built on a foundation of reusable templates you create once and deploy unlimited times. Here's how to find them:

Step 1: Open the GoHighLevel Mobile App
Download the official GoHighLevel mobile app (version 3.98.1 or later) on your iOS or Android device. Log in with your agency credentials.

Step 2: Navigate to the Estimates Section
From the main dashboard, tap the menu icon and look for "Estimates" or "Sales Tools." The exact placement depends on your account setup, but it's typically grouped with other client-facing features like invoices and proposals.

Step 3: Access Your Templates Library
Once in the Estimates section, you'll see two tabs: "Templates" and "Estimates." The Templates tab shows all pre-built estimate templates your team has created. These are your starting point for rapid estimate generation.

Step 4: Choose Your Template
Tap on any template to preview it. Templates display your company branding, pricing structure, line items, payment terms, and any attachments (like scope documents or service agreements).

💡 Pro Tip

Create multiple templates for different service offerings (e.g., "SEO Audit," "Website Design," "Monthly Marketing Management"). Your field team will spend less time customizing and more time closing deals.

Setting Up Your First Estimate Template

Before your team can send mobile estimates, you need to create templates on the desktop platform. Here's the process:

Create Templates on Desktop
Log into your GoHighLevel account from a computer. Navigate to Settings or the Estimates builder. You'll define:

  • Template Name: Use clear, descriptive names ("HVAC Service Call," "Plumbing Repair Estimate", etc.)
  • Line Items: Pre-populate products and services with standard pricing. Include quantity, unit price, and total for each line.
  • Branding: Add your company logo, colors, and contact information. GoHighLevel pulls your brand settings automatically.
  • Payment Terms: Set default payment due dates, deposit requirements, and accepted payment methods.
  • Custom Attachments: Upload scope of work documents, warranties, or service agreements that auto-attach to every estimate.
  • Direct Payments: Enable payment collection directly from the estimate (clients can pay via card, ACH, or other methods).

Enable Workflow Automation (Optional but Powerful)
GoHighLevel allows you to trigger workflows when estimates are sent, viewed, or accepted. For example:

  • Automatically send a follow-up text if the estimate isn't viewed within 24 hours
  • Create a task for your sales rep when the client accepts the estimate
  • Trigger an invoice creation once the estimate is approved

These workflows keep your sales process moving even when your team isn't manually checking in.

Creating and Sending Estimates from Your Phone

Once templates are set up, generating an estimate on the go takes 60-90 seconds. Here's the exact workflow:

Step 1: Open a Contact or Create a New One
From the mobile app, navigate to Contacts and select the prospect you're quoting. If they're a new contact, create a quick contact card with their name, phone, and email.

Step 2: Tap "Create Estimate"
In the contact's profile, tap "Create Estimate" or the estimates icon. This launches the estimate builder.

Step 3: Select Your Template
Choose from your pre-built templates. GoHighLevel automatically populates the estimate with your template's branding, line items, and payment terms.

Step 4: Customize for This Client
Adjust quantities, pricing, or add/remove line items as needed. You can also edit the estimate name, due date, and notes specific to this job.

Step 5: Preview and Send
Preview the estimate to confirm everything looks correct. Then send directly to the client via email, text, or WhatsApp. The estimate generates a trackable link—you'll see when they open it and if they accept.

Key Benefits of Mobile Estimate Templates

1. Consistency Across Your Team
Every estimate uses the same branding, pricing, and terms. No more variations in how different team members present services. Clients see a unified, professional image every time.

2. Speed to Close
Estimates that are sent within hours—not days—dramatically improve close rates. You're responding while the client is still thinking about the problem and comparing your solution to competitors.

3. Reduced Errors
Pre-built templates eliminate typos, wrong pricing, and missing line items. Your team fills in the basics; the template handles the rest accurately.

4. Payment Acceleration
Direct payment links embedded in mobile estimates mean clients can pay immediately. Some estimates collect deposits right there on the phone, further speeding up cash flow.

5. Complete Visibility
GoHighLevel tracks every estimate: when it was sent, opened, how long the client viewed it, and whether they accepted. This data helps your sales team prioritize follow-ups and understand conversion rates.

6. Scalability Without Overhead
Whether you're sending 10 estimates a week or 100, templates keep quality consistent and time investment minimal. Your team scales without adding admin work.

Best Practices for Field Teams

Always Take a Photo of the Job Site
Attach photos to your estimate context (in the notes or as a custom document). This reminds the client exactly what they're quoting for and reduces "I don't remember this conversation" objections.

Customize the Estimate Title and Notes
Use specific job descriptions: "Roof Inspection—123 Oak Street" instead of "Estimate #1234." Personalization increases client perception of value and makes follow-ups easier.

Set Clear Due Dates
Use templates with a default due date (e.g., 5 business days), but adjust based on urgency. Time-sensitive estimates close faster than open-ended ones.

Bundle Workflows with Estimates
If the client doesn't accept within 48 hours, trigger an automated follow-up text from your sales rep. GoHighLevel's workflows make this hands-off once set up.

Review Analytics Weekly
Check your estimate dashboard for trends: Which templates convert best? How long do clients typically take to respond? Use these insights to refine your templates and follow-up timing.

Train Your Team on Template Selection
Brief your field staff on which template to use for which situation. A 5-minute onboarding conversation prevents confusion and ensures consistent application.

Mobile estimates in GoHighLevel represent a fundamental shift in how service-based agencies and field teams operate. Instead of waiting until the office, your team responds to client needs immediately. Estimates are professional, consistent, and trackable. Payment collection happens faster. And your CRM captures the entire interaction automatically.

For agencies managing multiple teams across multiple job sites, mobile estimates create a massive efficiency gain—and a competitive advantage.
